description: Cross-domain dependency edges and composition rules for Domains VII-X (Mapping, Unification, Emergence, Synthesis)

edges.description: Categories are built on sets of objects and morphisms
Functors generalize group homomorphisms to arbitrary categories
Functors generalize linear maps between vector spaces to maps between categories
Functors are the categorical generalization of algebraic homomorphisms
Natural transformations are morphisms between functors
The Yoneda lemma characterizes objects in a category by their morphisms
Yoneda embedding is a functor from a category to its presheaf category
Shannon entropy is defined in terms of probability distributions
Continuous (differential) entropy uses integration over probability densities
Fourier transform generalizes Fourier series from periodic to aperiodic signals
The Fourier transform is defined as an integral transform
Fourier transform maps to complex-valued frequency domain via e^(iwt)
Nyquist-Shannon sampling theorem requires Fourier analysis of bandwidth
FFT is an efficient O(n log n) algorithm computing the discrete Fourier transform
Bayes' theorem follows directly from the axioms of probability
Markov chains are stochastic processes defined on probability spaces
Markov chain transitions are represented as matrix-vector multiplication
Convolution theorem states convolution in time becomes multiplication in frequency domain
CLT explains why the normal distribution is ubiquitous: sums of independent variables converge to it
U(1) electromagnetic gauge symmetry is a Lie group
SU(2) weak isospin symmetry is a non-abelian Lie group
SU(3) color symmetry of QCD is a non-abelian Lie group
Gauge fields are connections on fiber bundles over vector spaces
Gauge transformations form a symmetry group that governs field interactions
Noether's theorem derives from variation of the action integral
Each continuous symmetry group yields a conservation law via Noether's theorem
Lagrangian mechanics uses variational principle to find extrema of the action
Higgs mechanism requires gauge symmetry to then spontaneously break it
Higgs field breaks SU(2) weak isospin symmetry to give mass to W and Z bosons
String action (Nambu-Goto/Polyakov) is a worldsheet area integral
String scale set by dimensional analysis relating tension to Planck length
AdS/CFT duality emerges from string theory in anti-de Sitter spacetime
Boundary CFT lives in a quantum Hilbert space dual to bulk gravity
T-duality and S-duality are complementary manifestations of string duality
Bekenstein-Hawking entropy connects thermodynamic entropy to information-theoretic entropy
Stability analysis of fixed points uses derivatives of the logistic map
Lyapunov exponents measure exponential rate of divergence via derivative along trajectories
Time-averaged Lyapunov exponent is computed as a limit of an integral
Fractal (Hausdorff) dimension extends the concept of dimension beyond integers
L-systems are formal grammars defined as sets of production rules on symbol alphabets
Turing machines are defined in terms of sets of states, alphabets, and transition functions
Halting problem undecidability is proved by contradiction (diagonalization argument)
Backpropagation is the chain rule applied to compute gradients through neural network layers
Each neural network layer is a linear transformation followed by a nonlinearity
Gradient descent is a first-order iterative optimization algorithm
Neural network weight spaces and activations live in vector spaces
Neural network layers are linear transformations composed with activation functions
Strange attractors arise in nonlinear dynamical systems described by ODEs (e.g., Lorenz system)
Undecidability of the halting problem is proved by self-referential contradiction
The Complex Plane framework generalizes the coordinate plane to classify mathematical domains
Domain positions are represented as complex numbers on the logic-creativity/embodied-abstract plane
Decomposition traces advanced concepts back to foundational primitives like Pythagoras
The unit circle is a foundational primitive that many advanced concepts decompose to
Functors provide the formal mechanism for bridging between mathematical domains
Natural transformations ensure cross-domain bridges preserve structural relationships
The through-line begins with counting (natural numbers) as the first mathematical act
The through-line passes through calculus as the language of change
The through-line culminates in abstract algebra as the study of structure itself
The through-line connects mathematics to machine learning as emergent computation
Dependency chains are directed paths in the category of mathematical primitives
Domain activation uses Bayesian inference to select relevant mathematical domains
Scale invariance in synthesis mirrors the self-similarity property of fractals
All mathematics emerges from axiom systems like ZFC, connecting synthesis to foundations
Convolutional neural networks apply the convolution operation from signal processing
Attention computes scaled dot-product similarity between query and key vectors
Feynman diagrams can be formalized as morphisms in a monoidal category
Renormalization regularizes divergent integrals in quantum field theory
Conservation laws (energy, momentum, charge) derive from Noether's theorem
Bias-variance decomposition is expressed in terms of expected values over data
Universal approximation theorem applies to continuous functions on compact sets

compositionRules.yields: Rate-distortion analysis: decompose signal into frequency components, then quantify minimum bits per component for lossy compression
compositionRules.example: Fourier transform decomposes an audio signal into frequency bands, Shannon entropy quantifies the minimum bits needed to represent each band at a target distortion level

compositionRules.yields: Spectral feature extraction: frequency-domain representations as neural network input features
conditions: Input data has meaningful frequency structure
compositionRules.example: Audio classification pipelines apply FFT to waveforms, then feed spectrograms into convolutional networks for speech recognition

compositionRules.yields: Bayesian learning via maximum a posteriori estimation optimized by gradient descent
compositionRules.example: MAP estimation adds a log-prior penalty to the loss function, then gradient descent optimizes the regularized objective to find the most probable parameters

compositionRules.yields: Fiber bundle classification: principal bundles as categorical objects with gauge groups as structure groups
conditions: Structure group is a Lie group
compositionRules.example: Categorifying gauge theory: principal G-bundles over a manifold form a category whose morphisms are gauge transformations

compositionRules.yields: Stochastic gradient descent: random mini-batch sampling creates a Markov chain over parameter space converging to optima
conditions: Dataset is large enough for mini-batching
compositionRules.example: SGD randomly samples mini-batches (Markov property: next batch depends only on current parameters), applying gradient updates that converge to a local minimum

compositionRules.yields: Conservation law derivation: symmetry of a differential equation yields a conserved quantity along solutions
compositionRules.example: Time-translation symmetry of the Lagrangian for a harmonic oscillator ODE yields conservation of energy via Noether's theorem

compositionRules.yields: Gauge field construction: promoting a global symmetry group to a local gauge symmetry generates interaction fields
conditions: Symmetry group is a Lie group
Matter fields transform under the group representation
compositionRules.example: Promoting global U(1) phase symmetry to local U(1) gauge symmetry requires introducing the electromagnetic field (photon) as a connection

compositionRules.yields: QCD bound-state calculations: solving for hadron energy levels using SU(3) color symmetry constraints
conditions: Energy scale is in the non-perturbative QCD regime
compositionRules.example: Quark model combines SU(3) color multiplet structure with quantum mechanical energy eigenvalue equations to predict hadron mass spectra

compositionRules.yields: Neural network training: backprop computes gradients, optimization algorithm updates weights to minimize loss
compositionRules.example: Backpropagation computes dL/dw for each weight via chain rule, then Adam optimizer uses these gradients with adaptive learning rates to minimize cross-entropy loss

compositionRules.yields: Fractal analysis: computing Hausdorff dimension by measuring how sets scale with distance-based covering
conditions: Set exhibits self-similarity at multiple scales
compositionRules.example: Box-counting dimension of the Koch snowflake: cover with boxes of side epsilon (using Euclidean distance), count boxes N(epsilon), then D = lim log(N)/log(1/epsilon)

compositionRules.yields: Stability classification: Lyapunov spectrum of an ODE system classifies trajectories as stable, periodic, or chaotic
compositionRules.example: Computing Lyapunov exponents of the Lorenz system dx/dt=sigma(y-x), dy/dt=rx-y-xz, dz/dt=xy-bz reveals positive exponent confirming chaotic dynamics

compositionRules.yields: Decidability analysis: using diagonalization arguments to prove computational problems are undecidable
conditions: Problem is formally defined as a language recognition task
compositionRules.example: Prove the halting problem is undecidable: assume a Turing machine H decides halting, construct machine D that contradicts H on input D, concluding no such H exists

compositionRules.yields: Probabilistic domain routing: using probability distributions over Complex Plane positions to activate relevant mathematical domains
conditions: Problem has been decomposed into domain-relevant features
compositionRules.example: Given a problem about wave interference, probability mass concentrates near the Waves domain position on the Complex Plane, with secondary activation of Perception for trig

compositionRules.yields: Functorial decomposition: systematically mapping advanced concepts to their foundational components via structure-preserving transformations
compositionRules.example: Decompose neural network training: backprop functor maps to chain rule, loss function maps to optimization, weight updates map to linear algebra, preserving the compositional structure

compositionRules.yields: Efficient spectral analysis: computing Fourier series coefficients in O(n log n) for periodic signals
conditions: Signal is sampled at regular intervals
Sample count is a power of 2 for radix-2 FFT
compositionRules.example: Analyze a musical chord: sample the waveform at 44.1kHz, apply FFT to recover the Fourier series harmonics, identify fundamental frequencies of each note

compositionRules.yields: Channel coding design: entropy determines source compression limit, channel capacity determines transmission rate limit
compositionRules.example: Design a communication system: source entropy H(X) sets minimum bits for compression, channel capacity C = max I(X;Y) sets maximum reliable transmission rate

compositionRules.yields: Spectral analysis of weight matrices: eigenvalue decomposition reveals learned feature directions and training dynamics
conditions: Weight matrices are square or can be analyzed via SVD
Network has reached a meaningful training state
compositionRules.example: Eigendecomposition of a trained layer's weight matrix reveals principal feature directions; eigenvalue magnitudes indicate feature importance for classification

compositionRules.yields: Holographic complexity: fractal structure of the boundary CFT encodes bulk gravitational degrees of freedom
compositionRules.example: The fractal-like structure of entanglement entropy in a 2D CFT at criticality encodes the geometry of the dual 3D AdS spacetime via the Ryu-Takayanagi formula

compositionRules.yields: Convergence analysis: Taylor expansion of the loss around a critical point determines convergence rate and step size bounds
conditions: Current point is near a local minimum
compositionRules.example: Second-order Taylor expansion L(w+dw) ~ L(w) + g^T dw + 0.5 dw^T H dw gives quadratic convergence rate, optimal step size 1/lambda_max(H)

compositionRules.yields: Dimensional Bayesian inference: incorporating physical dimension constraints as prior information in parameter estimation
compositionRules.example: Estimating gravitational constant: Bayesian prior constrains G to have dimensions m^3/(kg*s^2), posterior updates from experimental measurements preserve dimensional consistency

compositionRules.yields: Mathematical pedagogy optimization: using gradient-based learning to optimize the presentation order along the through-line
compositionRules.example: Model student learning as a neural process: backpropagate from assessment outcomes through the dependency graph to identify which foundational concepts need reinforcement

compositionRules.yields: Convolutional neural network theory: convolution theorem explains why CNNs learn translation-equivariant features efficiently
conditions: Input has spatial or temporal structure
compositionRules.example: CNN filters perform spatial convolution; by the convolution theorem, this is multiplication in frequency domain, explaining why CNNs naturally learn frequency-selective features

compositionRules.yields: Information-theoretic bounds on spacetime: maximum entropy of a region scales with boundary area, not volume
conditions: Gravitational system is in thermal equilibrium
compositionRules.example: Bekenstein bound: maximum information content of a spherical region is S_max = A/(4*l_P^2) in Planck units, connecting Shannon information to spacetime geometry

compositionRules.yields: Markov decision process optimization: RL agent learns optimal policy over Markov state transitions
compositionRules.example: Q-learning agent navigates a grid world: state transitions form a Markov chain, Bellman equation propagates expected rewards backward through the chain to learn optimal actions
